description: Exception counts for {1,2,k} are not part of the current finding and are not provided in the supplied data. To confirm or fit growth at standard N and to obtain errors for the fit, additional enumeration for k up to at least 15 and at fixed N is required.
action_taken: Fitted two growth models to {1,2,k} exception counts at N=10^11 for k=5..10 (6 data points, all at consistent scale). Power law E(k) = C*k^b fits best (R^2=0.9999, AIC=81.4) with b=10.24 (95% CI [10.01, 10.46]). Exponential E(k) = C*a^k also fits well (R^2=0.9998, AIC=87.4) with a=3.024 (95% CI [2.91, 3.14]). Power law is preferred by AIC (delta=6.0). Exception counts: k=5: 374, k=6: 1834 (CLOSED), k=7: 7178 (CLOSED), k=8: 23590, k=9: 77109, k=10: 228514. Analysis script: scripts/experiments/zaremba-density/analyze_remediations.py.
